# Chase AI Habitat — Development Context

Author: Chase Arenella

This document provides the architectural context required for AI systems or developers working on the Habitat codebase.

The purpose of this document is to prevent accidental overwriting of system components when extending the project.

---

# Project Overview

Chase AI Habitat is a multi-agent cognitive system designed to function as an evolving AI organism that assists Chase Arenella with research, ideation, and exploration.

The system operates as a modular AI environment composed of independent agents coordinated by a central kernel.

Agents perform autonomous cognition cycles and generate discoveries that are stored within the Habitat knowledge layer.

A UI layer visualizes the internal state of the Habitat.

---

# System Architecture

The Habitat system is composed of four primary layers.

Kernel Layer  
Coordinates cognition cycles and orchestrates agents.

Agent Layer  
Autonomous agents responsible for research, exploration, and idea generation.

Knowledge Layer  
Stores discoveries, research notes, and system memory.

UI Layer  
Flask web interface used to visualize Habitat activity and interact with the system.

---

# Current UI Pages

/agents  
Displays the active agents within the Habitat.

/cognition  
Displays the cognition cycle timeline.

/chat  
Future interface allowing conversation with Habitat agents.

---

# Development Rules

These rules must always be followed when extending the project.

1. Never remove existing functionality unless explicitly instructed.
2. Never overwrite existing files without confirming architecture.
3. Always extend the system modularly.
4. When updating code files, provide FULL FILE replacements.
5. Assume existing pages must remain functional.
6. Never rename existing files unless explicitly instructed.
7. If uncertain about architecture, ask before modifying.

---

# Current System Status

The Habitat kernel is operational.

Agents run cognition cycles and generate discoveries.

The UI displays:

• Agent visualization  
• Cognition cycle timeline  
• Cyberpunk themed interface

Current development focus:

1. Chat interface
2. idea bubble visualization
3. agent cognition visualization
4. autonomous agent creation
